seedRoll.description: Quiet wetland wilderness — reed beds, drowned ruins, things half-sunk in the clay. Nobody settles here; the marsh keeps what it takes.
Painted canyon country baked to the bone. Dramatic tiered mesas, tomb-riddled badlands, no soft ground anywhere.
A roughed-up 1024-block cluster of gravel, basalt and ash where it all went wrong. Dead workings, broken portals, stone ruins — nobody could live here, and nothing organised survives.
Deep old-growth forest with roots in everything. Overgrown manors and spider dens under a closed canopy; a mansion looms near the border.
Nature won. Jungle-swallowed ruins and infested temples in compressed, tangled terrain — the wild reclaimed every wall.
The breadbasket. Rolling prairie and sunflowers, villages and taverns close to spawn, honest mineshafts — a place to live, not to fight.
Blossom cliffs over white stone — wisteria, sakura and chapels on the bluffs. Scenic first, gentle everything.
Gloomy cedar woodland where the fog sits low. Graveyards, chapels and a manor that should probably be left alone.
The empty deep mire. Sunken ruins and a mausoleum in the murk; too wet for roads, too quiet for company.
Broad green valleys with hamlets in the folds. Pastoral, generous, a little whimsical.
Wide river country crossed at white cliffs — fords, lighthouses and farms along the water.
Steep pine cliffs and fire towers. Dramatic conifer country that punishes a careless step.
Rolling chalk downs in heather and carnation. Villages in the hollows, nothing sinister for miles.
Craggy quarry country — bare spires, engineers' towers and old workings in the stone.
Ash-grey savanna under a volcanic sky. Abandoned camps and scorched tombs; the grove remembers the fire.
Amethyst rainforest and emerald peaks — a jewel-box world with wizard towers, not war.
Moonlit spruce dark. Graveyards, manors, a mansion deep in — the woods keep their own counsel.
Rain-soaked taiga, moss on everything, ruins rotting under the pines.
Autumn woods strewn with ruins — every clearing has bones of something older. Rich pickings, no living towns.
Broad warm shallows and mirage isles. Wrecks and drowned ruins under clear water; a swimmer's world.
Serene oasis pools glowing at dusk. A hermit's paradise — shrines yes, towns no.
A tiny, exhausted dust plain. Outcasts and buried ruins; the wind owns everything else.
Outpost country gone feral — pillager lookouts, bandit towers and campsites contest every hill.
Frozen channels between ice sheets. Igloos and cold dungeons; the sea is the road.
A void where a frozen world used to be — the biomes remain, the ground does not. The disintegration finished here.
A sculk-eaten void. The deep dark outlived the world it consumed.
A basalt-and-soul void — the nether's kind of ending, without the nether's mercy.
Classic nether halls — fortress corridors close to spawn, brick and fire all the way down.
Crimson root-country; forges and fungus in the red haze.
Basalt drama — pillars, bridges and a bastion in the columns.
A keep-world: bastions and blackstone walls from spawn. Small, fortified, expensive.
Industrial lava flats — pipelines and reactors over the melt.
The sanctum is the point: Incendium's cathedral dungeons saturate a small hard world.
Rolling ember meadows (overworld-shaped nether) — piglin villages in the geyser fields.
Warped groves and a greatsword in the fog — the nether's strange, quiet corner.
Soul-sand meadows and grave fields; the dead are the crop.
A toxic, dense little hell — ruined labs and reactors in the blight. Loot escalates with the danger.
Warped corruption spreading over a mesa world — teal fungus over red rock.
A vault of crying obsidian: castle dungeons and a sanctum at the edge of a small dense world.
Bones of giants in soul sand, with sculk creeping in. Dense, hard, deeply wrong.
An ancient buried age — nether ruins and towers of a civilisation the lava took.
Serene glowing caverns — lush moss under the nether's roof, quartz light, no armies.
Shroomlight groves and mushroom fields — the nether's gentlest rooms.
The gilded pit: a bastion at spawn and gold in every wall. Greed pays and costs.
Narrow bridged chasms — quartz spans over the wail.
The forge itself — crimson forges and reactors in a small brutal deep.
A vast glacial waste. Igloos and ice dungeons in an emptiness that goes on and on.
Temple-hunting waters — monuments, ocean fortresses and drowned dungeons everywhere you dive.
A snowbound island world — fir forests, cold dungeons, one hardy village.
A tiny abyss with a monument at its heart. Guardians own the water.
The pale desolate reach — snow ruins and little else. Beautiful, briefly.
Amethyst spires and wizard towers in violet haze — vertical, arcane, hostile to farmers.
Amplified madness — cliffs beyond reason, keeps on the peaks.
The deep dark as a surface world — ancient cities, sculk dungeons and mushroom light. Hard, rich, claustrophobic.
A flat blank canvas for building. Peaceful by decree.
Cherry-blossom gardens: sakura and wisteria at spawn, villages and taverns among the petals. Peaceful, never boring.
Untouched wilds that whisper — redwoods, moon groves, abandoned camps of those who didn't stay.
Vast desert kingdoms — walled villages, sand dungeons and a keep on the horizon.
The warm hearth in a frozen world — snowy villages and taigas with dungeons out in the cold.
The mountain crossing — guide posts and fire towers marking passes between dramatic highlands.
A compressed jungle gauntlet — infested temples and webbed dungeons in terrain that fights back. Dense and merciless.
The gauntlet: brutal peaks, ancient cities, trial chambers and a keep — every reward guarded. The hardest overworld there is.
Nether biomes on overworld noise — a nexus where the crimson bleeds through. Fortresses under an open sky.
Soul-sand drifting into shadow — the nether's graveyard on overworld bones, edged with the end's dark.
Floating garden islands — sakura and skylands adrift in a void slowly winning.
Burning islands in a void sea — nether archipelago on end-island bones.
The endless expanse — large biomes, big civilisation, room for a coliseum and a mansion at the far edge.
Citadel country — end cities and phantom citadels saturate the highlands.
A tiled quartz-and-void expanse — monoliths and archways in geometric silence.
Lonely pillars in the void — spires, a dragon's skeleton, nothing else.
The maw: sculk and shadowland in solid end terrain — the catalyst is still feeding.
Crumbling solid end — crystal peaks shearing into shadow, crashed ships in the rubble.
The red monument — scarlet mountains bleeding into end barrens around a monolith.
Fractured halls — gate fortresses and enderkeeps in broken crystal country.
The main overworld — safe, fairly vanilla spawn with interesting features nearby to kick off the journey; a real world players want to live in, with hard dungeons waiting near the border.
The nether — 1:8 travel makes its playable disc small, so it earns its keep: fortresses and bastions in reach, Incendium's sanctum near the rim.
The end — endgame space. Cities and citadels within honest reach of the gateways.
Paradise Lost — the skylands. Serene island heights with towers, vaults and a palace worth the climb.
